No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to poverty eradication or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The lesson '我生病了' (I was sick) provides basic communication skills for health-related situations, aligning with managing personal health risks (Target 3.d).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (4.3), enhances literacy (4.6), and develops relevant communication skills (4.4), fostering cultural diversity appreciation (4.7).
evidence: enhance learners' listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Language proficiency can be considered a relevant skill for employment, indirectly supporting productive employment and decent work (Target 8.5).
evidence: enhance learners' listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Learning communication skills for daily life and community interaction fosters social inclusion for all, supporting Target 10.2.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Lessons on navigation ('How do I get there?') and community interaction ('Welcome to the neighborhood') support functioning in human settlements (Targets 11.2, 11.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to sustainable consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The explicit mention of 'Respect Copyright' directly supports promoting the rule of law and access to justice (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: As a language course for international students, it inherently fosters international cooperation and knowledge sharing, supporting Target 17.6.
